top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
83ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour de Pologne Amatorów. Train it until it feels boring.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour de Pologne Amatorów's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
7,200ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 87 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
At Tour de Pologne Amatorów, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 87 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,200ft of climbing on paved Polish roads through Tatra Mountain does to your power file on race day.
Tour de Pologne Amatorów's paved Polish roads through Tatra Mountain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
The aerobic base that lets you ride paved Polish roads through Tatra Mountain at tempo for 87 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Tempo is Tour de Pologne Amatorów's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 87 miles on paved Polish roads through Tatra Mountain. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Tour de Pologne Amatorów’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and G Spot to prepare you for 87 miles of paved polish roads through tatra mountain foothills in Southern Poland (route varies — typically Krakow area / Tatra Mountain foothills).
